name: tidy
description: Tidy-as-you-touch: when you edit a file, format it and surface linter findings — fixing what's safe — flag it for decomposition when it grows past a size budget, and hold a subtractive posture (reuse before create, delete what a change makes redundant) so an actively-worked project converges toward clean code and trends down in complexity over time, scoped to the file you touched. Files over budget also surface automatically at session start (a light distill) — no manual trigger. The standard goes quiet (one-line re-anchor) once it's recorded in the project's CLAUDE.md. The /tidy:distill command runs the full read-only whole-project weight report on demand. Targets Go (goimports/gofumpt + golangci-lint) and web (eslint incl. jsx-a11y, stylelint — shifting Lighthouse a11y/best-practices to edit time); other languages no-op gracefully.
